name: merge-studio
description: A beautiful 3-way merge conflict editor for VS Code & Cursor: resolve conflicts with color ribbons and one-click accept arrows, a conflicts dashboard, undo history, and a precise side-by-side diff — with optional one-click hand-off to an installed JetBrains IDE.

enumDescriptions: Launch the real installed JetBrains IDE merge window (exact 1:1).
Use the built-in embedded merge editor (no external IDE needed).
jbMerge.conflictResolver.description: Which resolver to use for conflicts. 'webview' uses the embedded editor; 'jetbrains' opens the real installed IDE.

enumDescriptions: Use the built-in embedded diff editor (no external IDE needed).
Open the diff in the real installed JetBrains IDE.
jbMerge.diffTool.description: Which tool the 'Merge Studio: Compare' command uses. 'jetbrains' opens the real IDE, falling back to the embedded diff if none is installed.

jbMerge.preferredIde.description: Which installed JetBrains IDE to launch. 'auto' picks the first one found.

jbMerge.jetbrainsPath.description: Explicit path to a JetBrains IDE launcher (overrides auto-detection). e.g. /Users/you/Applications/WebStorm.app/Contents/MacOS/webstorm

jbMerge.autoOpen.description: Automatically open conflicted files with the selected resolver (supersedes the built-in merge editor).

walkthroughs.title: Get Started with Merge Studio
walkthroughs.description: Resolve merge conflicts and compare files in a beautiful three-pane editor, built into VS Code.

steps.title: Meet Merge Studio
steps.description: **A beautiful three-pane merge editor, built into VS Code.** Left is yours, center is the result you commit, right is theirs — wired with color ribbons and accept arrows. Includes a precise side-by-side diff. No external app required.

media.altText: Merge Studio — the embedded three-pane merge editor with connecting color ribbons.

steps.title: Try the three-pane merge
steps.description:
**Open a ready-made sample conflict and resolve it — no setup.** Click the accept arrows in the gutter ribbons to pull a side into the center result. Edit the result freely; alignment re-flows live as you type.

[Open sample merge](command:jbMerge.openDemo)

media.altText: Three-pane merge editor — yours, result, theirs — joined by accept arrows and color ribbons.

steps.title: Try the side-by-side diff
steps.description:
**Open a sample change in the side-by-side diff.** Panes align line for line, with intra-line highlights on exactly what moved.

[Open sample diff](command:jbMerge.openDemoDiff)

media.altText: Side-by-side diff with line-aligned panes and intra-line change highlights.

steps.title: Resolve your own conflicts
steps.description:
**Point Merge Studio at your repo.** Conflicted files open in the three-pane editor automatically after a merge or rebase, and the conflicts list shows every affected file so you can work through them one by one.

[Show my conflicts](command:jbMerge.showConflicts)

media.altText: Conflicts list showing every conflicted file left by a merge or rebase.

steps.title: Optional: open in a JetBrains IDE
steps.description: Prefer a JetBrains IDE? Set `jbMerge.conflictResolver` to `jetbrains` and Merge Studio opens conflicts directly in your installed IntelliJ, WebStorm, PyCharm, or other JetBrains IDE.

media.altText: The three-pane merge editor — Merge Studio can hand any merge off to an installed JetBrains IDE.

editor/title.when: config.git.enabled && !git.missing && resource in git.mergeChanges && git.activeResourceHasMergeConflicts && jbMerge.ideAvailable

virtualWorkspaces.description: Merge Studio requires a local git repository on disk.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: In Restricted Mode the workspace cannot override the JetBrains IDE launcher path (jbMerge.jetbrainsPath); everything else works.
